title: C2 — Bilingualize 5 i18n classes across three skills (roll-up template / errors / confirms / dry-run headers / AskUserQuestion header+question)
description: Add bilingual rendering (English + zh-CN) for 5 i18n classes in all three skills: (1) roll-up review/confirm templates, (2) error/warning messages, (3) confirmation prompts, (4) dry-run/diff table headers, (5) AskUserQuestion header+question text. AskUserQuestion options arrays remain English (routing keys). Add 'AskUserQuestion i18n Policy' chapter explaining value vs label distinction. Render language is chosen by knowledge_language from fabric-config.

files.target: All 5 i18n classes + AskUserQuestion i18n Policy chapter
files.change: Add zh-CN variants of every roll-up template + error msg + confirm prompt + dry-run header + AskUserQuestion header/question; keep options[] English. Add 'AskUserQuestion i18n Policy' chapter explaining: question + header → translate per knowledge_language; options[] → English routing keys
Mirror bilingual treatment; preserve all protected tokens
Mirror bilingual treatment; preserve all protected tokens

implementation: For each of 3 skills, enumerate user-facing string sites in each of the 5 classes (use grep for 'Confirm:' / 'Error:' / 'Warning:' / 'Dry run:' / 'AskUserQuestion' markers)
Draft canonical bilingual pattern: 'IF knowledge_language === "zh-CN": <Chinese variant>; ELSE: <English variant>' inline in markdown, OR a single bilingual template with `[en] / [zh]` lines

Draft 'AskUserQuestion i18n Policy' chapter (canonical wording shared across three skills): 'question and header fields are user-facing prose → translate per knowledge_language. options[] entries are routing keys consumed by the skill state machine → ALWAYS English regardless of language config.'
Apply bilingual templates to all 5 classes in all 3 templates; preserve protected tokens verbatim
Three-mirror sync: copy templates → .claude → .codex; verify byte-identity

reference.pattern: Conditional bilingual rendering driven by knowledge_language; English-only routing keys
reference.examples: AskUserQuestion options carry routing semantics — must stay English (e.g. 'approve','reject','modify','defer') even when label/header translate to Chinese

criteria: All 5 i18n classes have zh-CN variants in all 3 templates
AskUserQuestion options[] arrays remain English (verified by grep for option-list patterns)
'AskUserQuestion i18n Policy' chapter present in all 3 templates with consistent wording
Three-mirror byte-identity preserved for all 9 SKILL.md files

rationale.chosen_approach: Inline bilingual templates with language-conditional rendering; English-only routing keys
alternatives_considered: External i18n JSON files (adds runtime dep + load complexity for skills)
Translate AskUserQuestion options too (breaks routing logic)
decision_factors: Skills run as LLM context — inline bilingual is most discoverable
Routing keys must be stable across languages
rationale.tradeoffs: Markdown files get longer; manageable because the 5 classes are bounded — accepted

manual_checks: Set knowledge_language=zh-CN and run a skill; verify Chinese prose surfaces
Check AskUserQuestion options[] remain English in all three skills
diff -q across all three mirror pairs
